notes: Level 6 manual review override shard for spell indexes 10-14 in generated discovery row order.

drawmijs-instant-summons::vision_light_s.issue: The Invisible object mark and invisible sapphire inscription are not typed as object visibility/marking state.
drawmijs-instant-summons::vision_light_s.recommendedTemplateChange: Add invisible object mark and linked-token inscription fields.

drawmijs-instant-summons::message_or_com.issue: Speaking the object's name is an activation requirement for the stored summons link, not a message or communication mechanic.

drawmijs-instant-summons::travel_or_plan.issue: Crushing the sapphire can teleport the object to the caster's hand regardless of physical or planar distance, but fails if another creature holds or carries it; runtime lacks linked-object teleportation and blocked-transport fields.
drawmijs-instant-summons::travel_or_plan.recommendedTemplateChange: Add linked object teleport, planar distance, and holder-blocked transport fields.

drawmijs-instant-summons::social_or_know.issue: If another creature holds or carries the object, the caster learns who that creature is and where it is currently located; runtime lacks holder identity/location knowledge fields.
drawmijs-instant-summons::social_or_know.recommendedTemplateChange: Add linked-object holder identity/location knowledge fields.

drawmijs-instant-summons::status_or_stat.issue: The target object gains an Invisible mark and the sapphire gains an invisible inscription of the object's name; runtime lacks linked status/state data for either item.
drawmijs-instant-summons::status_or_stat.recommendedTemplateChange: Add linked item mark/inscription state fields.

drawmijs-instant-summons::conditional_en.issue: The spell ends when the object is summoned, and the runtime prose also notes Dispel Magic or similar effects on the sapphire end the spell; these ending conditions are not typed.
drawmijs-instant-summons::conditional_en.recommendedTemplateChange: Add linked-token destruction/use and dispel-on-token ending fields.

druid-grove::choice_or_mode.issue: The caster chooses warded effects, immune friends, a password, fog/undergrowth square placement, up to four tree guardians, and one of several extra magical effects; runtime lacks mode and per-effect choice fields.
druid-grove::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add multi-effect ward mode and immunity/password choice fields.

druid-grove::multi_instance_or_split_tar.issue: The spell can animate up to four trees and fill any number of 5-foot squares with different effects; runtime lacks split square/tree targeting fields.
druid-grove::multi_instance_or_split_tar.recommendedTemplateChange: Add split-square and multi-tree targeting fields.
druid-grove::multi_instance_or_split_tar.closedReason: Closed the multi-tree half by adding `animated_tree` to the target-instance vocabulary and recording Druid Grove as up to four persistent independently tracked tree guardians in structured markdown and runtime targeting.instanceAllocation. The split 5-foot square selection and ward/environment mechanics remain open under Druid Grove's terrain, choice/mode, environmental, ward, summon/control, message, and conditional-ending rows.

druid-grove::terrain_or_surface.issue: Outdoor/underground area eligibility, building/structure exclusion, fog squares, undergrowth squares, movement-cost fog, entangle-like undergrowth, spike-growth area, and wind are all prose-only.

druid-grove::message_or_communication.issue: The spoken password grants immunity, and grove guardians obey spoken commands while in the area; runtime lacks password immunity and command surfaces.
druid-grove::message_or_communication.recommendedTemplateChange: Add password immunity and spoken guardian command fields.

druid-grove::created_object_or_structure.issue: Buildings and other structures are excluded from the affected area; this is area eligibility/exclusion, not a created object or structure.
druid-grove::created_object_or_structure.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under terrain_or_surface or targeting eligibility.

druid-grove::summon_or_controlled_entity.issue: Animated tree guardians use awakened tree statistics, have altered speech/bark properties, fight intruders, obey spoken commands, cannot leave the ward, and reroot if possible when the spell ends; runtime lacks animated guardian entity/control fields.
druid-grove::summon_or_controlled_entity.recommendedTemplateChange: Add animated tree guardian entity and command fields.

druid-grove::ward_alarm_or_trigger.issue: The warded area radiates magic, Dispel Magic removes one effect at a time, non-immune creature entry triggers guardian fighting, and repeated yearly casting makes the spell last until dispelled; runtime lacks ward lifecycle and trigger fields.
druid-grove::ward_alarm_or_trigger.recommendedTemplateChange: Add ward subeffect dispel, intruder trigger, and permanence fields.

druid-grove::resource_or_consumption.issue: The detector matched fog movement cost; this is terrain movement cost, not resource consumption.

druid-grove::healing_or_restoration.issue: The detector matched obscuring fog and immunity prose; no healing or restoration mechanic is present.

druid-grove::environmental_change.issue: Fog, grasping undergrowth, animated trees, wind, spike growth, and harmless/soft variants for immune creatures alter the warded environment but are not typed.

druid-grove::conditional_ending.issue: Individual ward effects can be removed by Dispel Magic, the whole spell ends only when all effects are gone, and animated trees lose magic/reroot when the spell ends; runtime lacks these ending conditions.
druid-grove::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add per-subeffect dispel and guardian ending fields.

druid-grove::aftermath_or_memory.issue: When the spell ends, animated trees take root again if possible; runtime lacks this aftermath state.

eyebite::choice_or_mode.issue: Asleep, Panicked, and Sickened are now represented as utility options and corresponding status effects.
eyebite::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: No additional mode field required for this pass.
eyebite::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by controlOptions and STATUS_CONDITION effects.

eyebite::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The Wisdom save and negates-condition outcome are now represented on each status option.

eyebite::sustain_or_recast_action.issue: The each-turn Magic action to target another creature is represented as a granted action.
eyebite::sustain_or_recast_action.recommendedTemplateChange: No additional sustain field required for this pass.

eyebite::vision_light_sound.issue: Initial targeting and repeat targeting require a visible creature within 60 feet, and Panicked can end when the target is 60 feet away and cannot see the caster; runtime lacks per-option sight-gated ending data.

eyebite::status_or_state_change.issue: Unconscious, Frightened, and Poisoned outcomes are now represented as status effects with option details.
eyebite::status_or_state_change.recommendedTemplateChange: No additional status field required for this pass.

eyebite::conditional_ending.issue: Wake-on-damage/shake, Panicked distance-and-sight ending, and cannot-retarget-a-creature-that-succeeded-against-this-casting are preserved in descriptions but not first-class ending/state fields.
eyebite::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add per-option ending and per-casting target-immunity fields.

find-the-path::choice_or_mode.issue: Path-choice guidance is the spell's information output and is now classified as an information utility.
find-the-path::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: No separate choice field required for this pass.

find-the-path::forced_movement.issue: The detector matched moving destination in the failure criteria; the spell provides route knowledge and does not move a target.

find-the-path::travel_or_planar_movement.issue: Same-plane destination eligibility, failure for other-plane/moving/unspecific destinations, and same-plane duration condition are not first-class fields.
find-the-path::travel_or_planar_movement.recommendedTemplateChange: Add route destination eligibility and same-plane travel-knowledge fields.

find-the-path::social_or_knowledge_effec.issue: Distance, direction, and most-direct-path knowledge are now classified under an information utility and preserved in the runtime description.
find-the-path::social_or_knowledge_effec.recommendedTemplateChange: No additional social/knowledge field required for this pass.

find-the-path::environmental_change.issue: The detector matched destination restrictions; the spell does not alter the environment.

flesh-to-stone::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: The initial Constitution save and Restrained/Petrified save branches are now represented as status effects.
flesh-to-stone::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: No additional save field required for this pass.

flesh-to-stone::repeat_save_or_recurring.closedReason: Flesh to Stone now records the nonconsecutive three-success/three-failure counters in structured Repeat Save Progression and runtime repeatSave.progression data.
flesh-to-stone::repeat_save_or_recurring.issue: The end-turn Constitution repeat save is represented, but the separate nonconsecutive three-success and three-failure counters remain description-only.

flesh-to-stone::vision_light_sound.issue: The detector matched visible target selection, already represented by lineOfSight targeting.

flesh-to-stone::healing_or_restoration.issue: Greater Restoration or similar magic can end the maintained Petrified condition, but runtime lacks removable-by-restoration fields.

flesh-to-stone::status_or_state_change.issue: Restrained and Petrified are represented, but successful initial save setting Speed to 0 until the start of the caster's next turn and permanent Petrified after full concentration are not first-class.
flesh-to-stone::status_or_state_change.recommendedTemplateChange: Add success-outcome speed stop and concentration-completion permanence fields.

flesh-to-stone::environmental_change.issue: The detector matched body hardening/restraint prose; this is target status, not an environmental change.

flesh-to-stone::conditional_ending.issue: Three successful repeat saves end the spell, three failed saves convert the state to Petrified, and full-duration concentration changes the ending/removal behavior; runtime lacks those conditional ending fields.
flesh-to-stone::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add save-count and concentration-completion ending fields.
